perturbation.original_text: 2.2.1 Sponsorship and Responsibilities. PB will be the sponsor of the Clinical Trials in the PB Territory. SFJ will be the sponsor of the Clinical Trials in the SFJ Territory. SFJ in the SFJ Territory, and PB in the PB Territory, will have all responsibilities of a sponsor as specified in Applicable Laws, except, in the case of the Phase 3 Trial in the European Clinical Trial Countries, that SFJ shall perform certain activities that are PB's responsibilities as sponsor as set forth in Exhibit G.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2.1 Sponsorship and Responsibilities. PB will be the sponsor of the Clinical Trials in the PB Territory. SFJ will be the sponsor of the Clinical Trials in the SFJ Territory. The **CRO** in the SFJ Territory, and PB in the PB Territory, will have all responsibilities of a sponsor as specified in Applicable Laws, except, in the case of the Phase 3 Trial in the European Clinical Trial Countries, that SFJ shall perform certain activities that are PB's responsibilities as sponsor as set forth in Exhibit G.
perturbation.explanation: Changed the text to introduce confusion regarding who holds the responsibilities of a "sponsor" in the SFJ Territory, creating uncertainty and potential conflict in responsibilities. Now the text says, the CRO in the SFJ Territory is the one taking all responsibilities as a sponsor. This in-text contradition occurs because the previous text has defined SFJ as the one who is supposed to be the sponsor in that area. The contradiction occurs as the term 'sponsor' in the original text is well defined as an entity, whereas the changed text brings up the entity of a CRO which is well known it's a sub-entity of a sponsor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2.1 Sponsorship and Responsibilities. PB will be the sponsor of the Clinical Trials in the PB Territory. SFJ will be the sponsor of the Clinical Trials in the SFJ Territory. The **CRO** in the SFJ Territory, and PB in the PB Territory, will have all responsibilities of a sponsor as specified in Applicable Laws, except, in the case of the Phase 3 Trial in the European Clinical Trial Countries, that SFJ shall perform certain activities that are PB's responsibilities as sponsor as set forth in Exhibit G.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly assigns sponsorship responsibilities to SFJ in the SFJ territory. The changed text replaces SFJ with the 'CRO' for these responsibilities, creating a direct contradiction. The contradicted text is actually the same as the changed text, as this change introduces an in-text contradiction that wasn't there previously, where 'sponsor' responsibilities are taken by a CRO, when the term 'sponsor' was well defined as SFJ earlier in the section.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly following the Effective Date, PB and SFJ will jointly establish and maintain a Clinical Trials master file for each Clinical Trial in the format as agreed upon by the JDC (each a "Clinical Trials Master File"). Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, neither PB nor SFJ will be permitted to delegate its rights and obligations pursuant to this Section 3.5.4 to any Permitted Third Parties without the prior approval of the JDC, except either Party may delegate its rights and obligations pursuant to this Section 3.5.4 to any of its Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly following the Effective Date, PB and SFJ will jointly establish and maintain a Clinical Trials master file for each Clinical Trial in the format as agreed upon by the JDC (each a "Clinical Trials Master File"). Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, either PB nor SFJ will be permitted to delegate its rights and obligations pursuant to this Section 3.5.4 to any Permitted Third Parties or to their Affiliates without the prior approval of the JDC, except either Party may delegate its rights and obligations pursuant to this Section 3.5.4 to any of its Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces uncertainty and in-text contradiction by stating neither PB nor SFJ can delegate these rights to any third parties or their Affiliates, yet in the end says they can delegate this rights to their Affiliates, making it in-text contradicting. This change introduces a direct contradiction within Section 3.5.4 regarding the delegation of rights and obligations related to the Clinical Trials Master File. The initial part of the sentence states that neither PB nor SFJ can delegate these rights to "any Permitted Third Parties or to their Affiliates", while the latter part of the sentence states that either Party may delegate these rights to "any of its Affiliates."
perturbation.contradicted_text: Promptly following the Effective Date, PB and SFJ will jointly establish and maintain a Clinical Trials master file for each Clinical Trial in the format as agreed upon by the JDC (each a "Clinical Trials Master File"). Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, either PB nor SFJ will be permitted to delegate its rights and obligations pursuant to this Section 3.5.4 to any Permitted Third Parties or to their Affiliates without the prior approval of the JDC, except either Party may delegate its rights and obligations pursuant to this Section 3.5.4 to any of its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. It initially states that neither PB nor SFJ can delegate rights to Permitted Third Parties or Affiliates without JDC approval. However, it then contradicts itself by stating that either party *can* delegate rights to its Affiliates, creating an internal inconsistency within the same section, as the changed text and contradicted text are the same.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will comply with the subject recruitment plan for the Clinical Trials, which will be established by each Party for their respective Territory, except in the case of the European Clinical Trial Countries which SFJ will be responsible for, and communicated to the JDC, for approval by the JDC not to be unreasonably withheld, within a reasonable period of time after the Effective Date not to exceed [***] of the Effective Date (the "Subject Recruitment Plan") in recruiting subjects to participate in the Clinical Trials.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will comply with the subject recruitment plan for the Clinical Trials, which will be established by each Party for their respective Territory, except in the case of the European Clinical Trial Countries which **PB** will be responsible for, and communicated to the JDC, for approval by the JDC not to be unreasonably withheld, within a reasonable period of time after the Effective Date not to exceed [***] of the Effective Date (the "Subject Recruitment Plan") in recruiting subjects to participate in the Clinical Trials.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduce in-text contradiction by alternating the responsible party for the Subject Recruitment Plan in the European Clinical Trial Countries from SFJ to PB. Previously and later in the text, the responsibility is attributed to SFJ, thus this alternate makes the text contradicting. It directly contradicts the established roles by assigning responsibility for the European Clinical Trial Countries' subject recruitment plan to PB instead of SFJ, creating ambiguity and potential disputes over responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties will comply with the subject recruitment plan for the Clinical Trials, which will be established by each Party for their respective Territory, except in the case of the European Clinical Trial Countries which **PB** will be responsible for, and communicated to the JDC, for approval by the JDC not to be unreasonably withheld, within a reasonable period of time after the Effective Date not to exceed [***] of the Effective Date (the "Subject Recruitment Plan") in recruiting subjects to participate in the Clinical Trials.
perturbation.justification: The changed text and the contradicted text are identical. Therefore, the contradiction arises within the changed text itself compared to the original text. While the explanation details a contradiction caused by altering the responsible party for the Subject Recruitment Plan in the European Clinical Trial Countries, the provided 'Contradicted Text' is the same as the 'Changed Text'. This indicates an error in the provided data, or the contradiction exists with the original text, not necessarily with another part of the document. However, based strictly on the given changed and contradicted texts, there is no new contradiction created between the sections, since they are the same. Thus, 'NO' is assigned. The fact that the changed text contradicts the original doesn't meet the criterion of creating a contradiction between different parts of the *current* document.
